items.definition_en: Algebra is the branch of mathematics that deals with symbols and the rules for manipulating them.
items.example_en: We're starting algebra next semester in math class.

items.definition_en: Geometry is the study of shapes, sizes, and the properties of space.
items.example_en: I enjoy learning about triangles and circles in geometry.

items.definition_en: Calculus is a branch of mathematics focusing on change and motion.
items.example_en: Calculus is a challenging subject for many high school students.

items.definition_en: Statistics is the science of collecting and analyzing numerical data.
items.example_en: We analyzed survey results in our statistics class.

items.definition_en: World literature refers to famous written works from various cultures around the world.
items.example_en: Our teacher assigned a novel for world literature this month.

items.definition_en: Ancient history is the study of people and events from early civilizations.
items.example_en: We are learning about Ancient Egypt in ancient history.

items.definition_en: Medieval history focuses on the period between the fall of Rome and the Renaissance.
items.example_en: Our project for medieval history was about castles.

items.definition_en: Earth science is the study of the planet, including rocks, weather, and oceans.
items.example_en: Earth science classes often include experiments with soil and water.

items.definition_en: Environmental science explores how humans impact the natural world.
items.example_en: We learned about recycling in environmental science.

items.definition_en: Microbiology is the study of tiny organisms called microbes.
items.example_en: In microbiology, we observed bacteria using a microscope.

items.definition_en: Botany is the branch of biology dealing with plants.
items.example_en: She chose botany for her science research project.

items.definition_en: Genetics is the study of how traits are passed from parents to offspring.
items.example_en: My teacher explained DNA in our genetics unit.

items.definition_en: Molecular biology studies the structure and function of molecules in living things.
items.example_en: Molecular biology helps us understand diseases at a cellular level.

items.definition_en: Political science examines government systems and political activity.
items.example_en: Alex plans to study political science at university.

items.definition_en: Psychology is the study of the human mind and behavior.
items.example_en: Psychology classes cover topics like memory and emotions.

items.definition_en: Sociology explores how societies and groups of people function.
items.example_en: We discussed family structures in sociology.

items.definition_en: Philosophy is the study of the nature of knowledge, reality, and existence.
items.example_en: We debated ethics in our philosophy class.

items.definition_en: Business studies teach about how companies work and how to manage them.
items.example_en: He chose business studies for his elective this year.

items.definition_en: Information technology is about using computers to store and process data.
items.example_en: We build websites in information technology class.

items.definition_en: Drama is the subject where students learn about acting and theater.
items.example_en: She played the lead role in drama class.
